transition: 兌 Duì (Joyous Lake) -> 無妄 Wú Wàng (Innocence)
verse_zh: 結網得解，受福安坐，終无患禍。
verse_en: The knotted net finds release; one receives blessing and sits at ease. In the end there is no trouble or calamity.
note: Blessing and ease -> a human figure at rest. The released net becomes a calm detail, not the subject.
prompt: Beneath a thatched eave framing the view, an old fisherman sits at ease on worn planks, a loosened net slack at his feet like unraveled ink threads. In the midground a calm inlet catches dawn light; a small boat drifts, its lantern a single amber spark. Reed banks and pale hills recede into mist beyond. Chinese ink painting.
